ctx.name: Bill
ctx.notes: Director is not got enough asdexperience.

focusPoints.label: Strategic impact at the director level
focusPoints.reason: No specific signal — performance & feedback for a director should focus on strategic impact.

focusPoints.label: Delegation to empower team leads
focusPoints.reason: No specific signal — at director level, effective delegation is crucial for scaling impact and team development.

focusPoints.label: Judgment on high-stakes decisions
focusPoints.reason: No specific signal — directors need to make sound decisions on tradeoffs; this is a core competency to explore.

focusPoints.label: Engagement with executive peers and stakeholders
focusPoints.reason: No specific signal — directors must manage relationships beyond their immediate team; worth discussing how this is going.

focusPoints.label: Clarity on director-level expectations
focusPoints.reason: Notes mention a lack of experience. Worth clarifying if there's any uncertainty about what success looks like at this level.

transcript.question: Before I share my view, how do you think the last stretch has gone?
What's something from the last quarter you're genuinely proud of — not what looks good, what actually was good?
How are your relationships with executive peers evolving?
What part of your current workload feels the least clear in terms of expectations or priorities?
How sustainable does your current pace feel for you?
What aspects of the director role feel most and least clear to you?
Where do you think you've fallen short of where you want to be?
What part of delegation feels most challenging for you right now?

notes: Director is not got enough asdexperience.
